title: Register optimizer documentation tool in procedure runtime
description:
Direct optimizer procedure runs fail during startup because feedback_alignment_optimizer.yaml calls `get_plexus_documentation(filename="optimizer-cookbook")`, but the embedded procedure runtime does not expose the documentation MCP tool in the toolset used for the run.

Expected behavior:
- Direct CLI optimizer procedure runs can load the optimizer cookbook.
- `get_plexus_documentation` is available whenever the procedure runtime registers the Plexus toolset used by optimizer procedures.
- No fallback path or duplicated cookbook content is added; the single documented tool path should work.

Evidence: `.codex/procedure-runs/blind-transfer-optimizer-20260501-151345.log` failed with Tool `get_plexus_documentation` not found.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implemented local fix for direct optimizer startup failure: filtered procedure MCP toolsets now always include the documentation category, matching the existing always-on think helper behavior. Added regression coverage that a filtered rubric_memory-only server still exposes get_plexus_documentation. Verification: pytest plexus/cli/procedure/test_mcp_transport.py plexus/cli/procedure/test_feedback_alignment_optimizer_config.py plexus/cli/procedure/test_procedure_optimize_cli_result.py -q (68 passed, warnings only from existing dependency deprecations). Not closing until reviewed/landed.
